folio.label: 159r
folio.l: But he was chast and no lechour
And swete as is the brambel flour
That beretú the red hepe
And so it fel vp on a day
For sothe as I ©ow telle may
Sire Thopas wold out ride
He wortú vp on his steede gray
And in his hond a launcegay
A longö swerd by his side
He pryketú thurgú a fair forest
Ther Inne is many a wylde best
ña bothe Buk and hare
And as he priketú Nortú and Est
I telle it ©ow hi~ had almest
Bitidde a sory kare
There spéngen erbes grete & smale
The lycoris and the cetewale
And many a clowe gylophre
And Notemuge to putte in ale
Wheither it be moyst or stale
Or for to leyn in coffre
The briddes songen it is no nay
The S¹hauk and the Popyngay
That ioye it was to heere
The thrustelcok mad eke hire lay
The wode dowe vp on a spray
She sangö ful loude & clere
Sire Thopas fel in loue longyngö
Al whan he herd , the thrustel syngö
& pryked as he were wood
His fair steede in his prekyngö
So swatte , æ me~ mygút hi~ wryngö
His sides were al blod
Sire Thopas eke so wery was
For prikyngö on the softe gras
So fiers was his corage
That doun he leid hi~ in that place
To make his steede som solace
And ©af hi~ good forage
O Seynte Mary benedicite
What eyletú this loue at me
To bynde me so sore
Me dremed al this nygút ¹de
An Elf Quene shal my le~man be
And slepe vnder my gore
An Elf Quene wil I loue I wys
For in this world no wo~man is
Worthy to be my make ,,
In towne
Alle othere wo~men I forsake
And to an Elf Quene I me take
By dale and eke by downe
In to his sadel he clom a noon

note.notetxt: 86The ink changes here from brown becomes ligh brown. This ink is used to the end of the manuscript.ODR

folio.l: And priketú ouer stile and ston
An Elf Quene for to espie
Til he so longe hatú ryde & gon
That he fond in a pryue won
The contreye of fairie
So wylde
For in that contrey was æäe noon
That to hi~ durst ride or gon

note.notetxt: 93-1the line was inserted with a different colour of ink.ODR

folio.l: Neither wyf ne childe
Til that æäe com a gret geaunt
His name was Sire Olifaunt
A ¹ilous man of dede
He seide child by Termagaunt
